name: Radiohead

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes a mature alternative catalog with stable streaming, strong vinyl and reissue demand, and relatively favorable long-term artist economics.

ownership.master_owner: Ownership is mixed across eras, with some releases carrying more artist leverage than major-label peers
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing value remains meaningful because core songs continue to travel across generations
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No broad catalog sale assumed in this estimate
ownership.notes: Radiohead likely captures more downside protection from catalog economics than many older acts because of stronger long-term leverage.

bio: Radiohead's catalog monetizes through deep listener loyalty, critical status, and songs that remain central to alternative-rock playlists.
notable_for: Art-rock experimentation, cult loyalty, and one of the strongest alternative catalogs of the modern era.
career_highlight: Their songs continue to earn through a combination of classic hits, album-driven listening, and long-tail discovery.
revenue_drivers: Deep catalog listening supports steady streaming income.
Classic tracks remain central to alternative and mood playlists.
Prestige catalog status helps keep the band relevant for long-term licensing.
